page: Amazon Web Services c7i.xlarge is a compute-optimized instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 2 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8488C processor running at 3.2 GHz. The instance is equipped with 8.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, yielding a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. It includes no local storage or GPUs, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate average single-core capabilities, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1845.0 and a stress-ng single-core score of 1990.39. However, multi-core performance is relatively weak, as shown by a Geekbench multi-core score of 4017.0 and a stress-ng multi-core score of 4030.98. Memory bandwidth is a notable bottleneck, with Passmark memory scores rated as poor and uncached read/write bandwidths hovering around 23 GB/sec. Conversely, the processor excels in text processing, achieving a strong Geekbench text processing score of 2335.0, and shows average performance in static web serving at 7.34 GB/sec and small-model LLM inference.

The c7i.xlarge offers a cost-efficient entry point for compute-bound tasks that do not require massive memory footprints or local storage. Its advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 and Intel AMX (Advanced Matrix Extensions), support specialized workloads like cryptography and low-latency vector math. However, the low memory-to-vCPU ratio and poor memory bandwidth make it less suitable for memory-intensive databases or heavy multi-threaded compiling. It is best suited for single-threaded or lightly multi-threaded compute tasks, text processing, static web serving, and lightweight machine learning inference.
description: Amazon Web Services c7i.xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs and 8.0 GB of DDR5 RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8488C processor running at 3.2 GHz on the Nitro hypervisor, it delivers average single-core performance but exhibits weaker multi-core and memory bandwidth capabilities. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs, relying on a 2 Gbps baseline network. It is best suited for text processing, static web serving, and lightweight compute workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c7i.xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 8.0 GB of DDR5 RAM, and a 2 Gbps network baseline, optimized for text processing and light compute tasks.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c7i.xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 4 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 8.0 GB DDR5 RAM, and average single-core performance.
tagline: A compute-optimized instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and DDR5 memory for text processing and lightweight single-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 3.2 GHz Intel Xeon 8488C processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs
Equipped with 8.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory at 5 GHz
Delivers strong performance in text processing and average single-core benchmarks
Features advanced CPU instruction sets including Intel AMX and AVX-512
Provides a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps with no local storage
Best suited for static web serving, text processing, and light inference workloads
